reason: The servant Ruth demonstrated loyalty to her mother-in-law Naomi by choosing to stay with her and accompany her back to Bethlehem, even though she had the option to return to her own people and gods. Ruth declared her commitment to Naomi by saying, 'Where thou goest, I will go; and where thou lodgest, I will lodge: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God.' This loyalty is evident throughout the text as Ruth remains steadfast in her devotion to Naomi, ultimately leading to her marriage to Boaz and the birth of a son who became part of the lineage leading to King David.
